perturbation.original_text: Dolphin shall charge DGT USD1.50 per delivered, completed photo. The price per unit shall be open to renegotiation - in response to competitive pricing pressures - upon receipt of written notice at any time by either party;
perturbation.changed_text: Dolphin shall charge DGT USD1.50 per delivered, completed photo. The price per unit is fixed and not subject to renegotiation under any circumstances;
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction. The original clause allows for price renegotiation based on market conditions with written notice. The modified clause explicitly states that the price is fixed and cannot be renegotiated, creating uncertainty about whether price changes are permissible, conflicting with the flexibility the original clause intended to provide. This will lead to problems when enforcing the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dolphin shall charge DGT USD1.50 per delivered, completed photo. The price per unit is fixed and not subject to renegotiation under any circumstances; Page 1 of 5 5.Dolphin will provide photo-editing services exclusively, at DGT option, when the number of downloaded photos to be edited by Dolphin totals 50,000 in a single year; 6.Dolphin acknowledges that it is illegal to copy or reproduce these photographs, including but not limited to electronic reproduction, without DGT expressed permission, and any violation will be subject to civil and criminal penalties.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allowed for price renegotiation based on competitive pressures with written notice. The changed text explicitly states that the price is fixed and not subject to renegotiation under any circumstances. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, and creates an in-text contradiction by presenting conflicting obligations. This fits the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition because the same clause is repeated, creating confusion on the enforceability of either clause.

perturbation.original_text: DGT shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement if Dolphin fails to meet its requirements and material obligation hereunder and only after DGT have given Dolphin written notice of such failure and only after Dolphin has not rectified such failure within 10 days of the notice.
perturbation.changed_text: DGT shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, and without providing any prior notice to Dolphin.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a conflict in the termination rights. Originally, DGT could only terminate for specific failures, with a 10-day notice and opportunity to rectify. The modification allows DGT to terminate at will, contradicting the original clause's stipulations. This makes it unclear under what conditions the agreement can be terminated by DGT, leading to potential disputes. This will lead to problems when enforcing the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DGT shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, and without providing any prior notice to Dolphin. Dolphin shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement only after DGT fails to meet a material obligation hereunder and only after Dolphin has given DGT written notice of such failure and only after the DGT has not rectified such failure within 30 days of the notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text grants DGT the right to terminate the agreement at any time, with or without cause, and without prior notice. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Dolphin can only terminate the agreement if DGT fails to meet a material obligation after a 30-day notice period and opportunity to rectify. This creates a clear inconsistency in the termination rights of both parties, making it unclear under what conditions the agreement can be terminated and thus contradicting the original intention of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Dolphin agrees to complete its photo-editing services within 14 days of receiving the original digital photo files. The completed files must meet DGT pre-defined standards for finished products;
perturbation.changed_text: Dolphin agrees to use its best efforts to complete its photo-editing services within a reasonable time of receiving the original digital photo files. The completed files must meet DGT reasonable standards for finished products, at Dolphin’s sole discretion;
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity and contradicts the original terms regarding completion time and standards. Originally, a 14-day deadline and DGT's pre-defined standards were set. The modified clause uses vague terms like "reasonable time" and introduces Dolphin's discretion in meeting "reasonable standards." This contradicts the specific requirements of the original, making it unclear what timeline and quality standards are actually enforceable. This will lead to problems when enforcing the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Dolphin agrees to use its best efforts to complete its photo-editing services within a reasonable time of receiving the original digital photo files. The completed files must meet DGT reasonable standards for finished products, at Dolphin’s sole discretion; 4.Dolphin shall charge DGT USD1.50 per delivered, completed photo. The price per unit is fixed and not subject to renegotiation under any circumstances; Page 1 of 5 5.Dolphin will provide photo-editing services exclusively, at DGT option, when the number of downloaded photos to be edited by Dolphin totals 50,000 in a single year; 6.Dolphin acknowledges that it is illegal to copy or reproduce these photographs, including but not limited to electronic reproduction, without DGT expressed permission, and any violation will be subject to civil and criminal penalties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces specific terms ('14 days', 'DGT pre-defined standards') with vague, subjective ones ('reasonable time', 'DGT reasonable standards, at Dolphin's sole discretion'). The contradicted text includes the same altered language, which does not negate the fact that the original, more specific clause elsewhere in the contract is now contradicted. This undermines the enforceability of the original, objective standards and creates a contradiction regarding the performance standards required of Dolphin.
